tender.title: Housing Related Floating Support Services
tender.description:
Medway's vision for Housing Related Support (HRS) is to improve the quality of life of vulnerable socially excluded people living in the borough through the delivery of preventative housing support services and to make Medway a place where people are able to achieve their potential for independent living.

Housing Support for vulnerable and socially excluded people in Medway has 3 identified overarching aims. These are:

• Delivering independence and preventing homelessness: this is through providing housing support in Supported Housing, or through Floating Support, giving people the opportunity to stabilise their lives and move on.
• Creating opportunities: this is through offering vulnerable and socially excluded people experiencing and/or threatened with homelessness, the chance to improve their outcomes and increase their independence.
• Providing effectively managed services and value for money: This is through delivering high quality, strategically relevant services that are well managed, cost effective, work well with other support and care services, and put service users at the heart of service delivery.

address.streetAddress: Medway Council, Gun Wharf, Dock Road

tender.title: Contract for the cleaning of West Suffolk Temporary Accommodation sites
tender.description:
The contract covers the cleaning of communal/ shared areas in West Suffolk temporary accommodation over five sites.

1.	St Andrews Street North - 3 hours per week
 
2.	26, Brentgovel Street - 1 hour per week
 
3.	34, Lake Avenue - 3 hours- per week
 
4.	1, The Avenue - 3 hours - per week
 
5.	100a College Heath Road - 2 hours  - per week
 
Any ad hoc additional deep / bio hazard cleans will be charged separately.

Bi-annual deep cleaning of ovens to be charged separately.

tender.title: Accommodation Based Housing Related Support Services
tender.description:
Medway's vision for Housing Related Support (HRS) is to improve the quality of life of vulnerable socially excluded people living in the borough through the delivery of preventative housing support services and to make Medway a place where people are able to achieve their potential for independent living.

Housing Support for vulnerable and socially excluded people in Medway has 3 identified overarching aims. These are:

• Delivering independence and preventing homelessness: this is through providing housing support in Supported Housing, with move on support where appropriate, giving people the opportunity to stabilise their lives and move on.
• Creating opportunities: this is through offering vulnerable and socially excluded people experiencing and/or threatened with homelessness, the chance to improve their outcomes and increase their independence.
• Providing effectively managed services and value for money: This is through delivering high quality, strategically relevant services that are well managed, cost effective, work well with other support and care services, and put service users at the heart of service delivery.

The tendering process will be conducted through the Kent Business Portal.

tender.title: A Framework for Care Experienced Young People offer of local accommodation and support
tender.description:
The council is seeking to run a procurement with Local Landlords and Support Providers, to offer their services via a Framework to the council.  The Framework will help achieve an improved and sustained local accommodation and support offer to care experienced young people. Applicants accepted onto the Framework will be in a position to apply through a mini competition to offer their service(s) of accommodation and/or support to our care experienced young people.  
The tender will be let as a framework consisting of two separate Lots as identified below.  Providers will be permitted to bid for a single Lot or for both Lots together:
Lot 1: Accommodation (excluding Support)  
Lot 2: Support only for Care leavers
The Framework will be established under the Light Touch Regime and therefore applies the flexibilities afforded under regulation 76 of the Public Contracts Regulations 2015. These flexibilities include the re-opening of the Framework to allow new entrants periodically. Given the nature of the supported accommodation and to maintain a viable framework, it is necessary to allow new entrants to enter the Framework periodically (at the discretion of the council and subject to service needs.) 
If you are interested in bidding for this opportunity please go to www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk and search under Herefordshire Council to access the tender documents and submit your bid.

tender.title: Tender for Housing Related Support in the Bradford District
tender.description:
City of Bradford Metropolitan District Council now seeks tenders for Housing Related Support for people: Facing multiple disadvantage including but not exclusively mental health and substance misuse challenges; Facing multiple disadvantage who are ex-offenders with a history of serious offending history; Leaving hospital who are homeless and facing multiple disadvantage; and who are 18-25, (and 16-17 in exceptional circumstances) and facing significant challenges.  

This award notice constitutes award to Lot 2, Multiple Disadvantage: Young People.  Remaining Lots 1 and 3-6 were not awarded due to lack of suitable bids received

Additional information: We are working with the Probation Service and hope to increase the level of funding to Lot 1 for additional capacity in the service to support women with a serious offending history in response to recognised need, as outlined in the needs assessment.

tender.title: Wiltshire Extra Care Housing
tender.description:
Wiltshire Extra Care Housing

Extra Care Housing (ECH) plays a vital role in the local health and care system.  Supporting more people to remain living independently in the community, whilst providing a safe environment to meet evolving and emerging care needs, which is a priority for the council. 

There are 4 affordable housing schemes in Wiltshire which are designated as Extra Care Housing (ECH) and funded by the council:
- Crammer Court
- Needham House
- Meadow Court
- Bell Orchard

tender.title: GBC - Strategic advice and financial modelling for affordable housing developments - AWARD
tender.description:
GBC is seeking to appoint a supplier for providing strategic advice and financial modelling for affordable housing developments. As part of its commitment to provide new affordable housing, GBC is bringing forward a portfolio of sites for redevelopment. 
      
      Work has been undertaken to develop scheme designs and, in some instances, planning approval has been obtained. Several other schemes are in the pipeline and it is envisaged that more schemes will follow.
      
      GBC intends to develop the projects itself, retaining the affordable housing created within the HRA. The assets created will be managed and maintained by GBC for the duration of their life. 
      
      GBC wants to test the viability of its schemes and is seeking to appoint a single suitably qualified and experienced firm with a demonstrable and up-to-date track record in advising Councils and / or Housing Associations on affordable and mixed-tenure housing projects.  
      
      The requirement is to provide strategic and financial advice to GBC in relation to its programme of affordable-led housing development. The requirement includes one initial piece of work but will run for up to a four year period , during which GBC will call-off work as required to help ascertain the viability of its development schemes.
      o Impact on general fund
      o Payback over thirty years
